content.problemStatement:
When Chef was born, his parents took him to the famous monk Doctor Strange to know whether he will land himself in heaven after his life or not. According to Strange, Chef will live for LLL years in total. If he wants to go to heaven, he must spend at least 50%50\%50% of his life years doing good deeds. He also shows them his future using a string SSS of length LLL where Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 means the iii-th year will be counted as bad as per the rule books of heaven and Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 means the iii-th year will be counted as good.

Also, Strange can use his special powers to make Chef end his life earlier than that planned by god, i.e, he can choose some L′L'L′ (1≤L′≤L1\leq L'\leq L1≤L′≤L) and make him live for only L′L' L′ years. Strange wants Chef to succeed, so if there is any choice of L′L'L′ that allows Chef to go to heaven, he will do so.

Tell whether Chef can go to heaven.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line contains a single integer LLL.
The second line contains a string SSS of length LLL, consisting of symbols `0` and `1`.
###Output
For each test case, output the answer in a single line: "YES" if Chef can go to heaven and "NO" if not (without quotes).

You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

###Constraints


1≤L≤1051 \leq L \leq 10^51≤L≤105
The sum of LLL over all tests does not exceed 10610^6106
###Subtasks

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: If Chef lives for the complete 222 years, he will have a total of 111 good year which is 1∗1002=50%\frac{1 * 100}{2} = 50\%21∗100​=50% of his life, and hence he will go to heaven.
Test case 2: There's no way Chef can go to heaven.
Test case 3: If Chef lives for 222 years, he will have a total of 111 good year which is 1∗1002=50%\frac{1 * 100}{2} = 50\%21∗100​=50% of his life, and hence he will go to heaven.
